title: Chapter XIII. 31–32.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It becomes us, dearly beloved, to keep in view the orderly connection of our Lord’s words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For after having previously said, but subsequently to Judas’ departure, and his separation from even the outward communion of the saints, “Now is the Son of man glorified, and God is glorified in Him;”—whether He said so as pointing to His future kingdom, when the wicked shall be separated from the good, or that His resurrection was then to take place, that is, was not to be delayed, like ours, till the end of the world;—and having then added, “If God is glorified in Him, God shall also glorify Him in Himself, and shall straightway glorify Him,” whereby without any ambiguity He testified to the immediate fulfillment of His own resurrection; He proceeded to say, “Little children, yet a little while I am with you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To keep them, therefore, from thinking that God was to glorify Him in such a way that He would never again be joined with them in earthly intercourse, He said, “Yet a little while I am with you:” as if He had said, Straightway indeed I shall be glorified in my resurrection; and yet I am not straightway to ascend into heaven, but “yet a little while I am with you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, as we find it written in the Acts of the Apostles, He spent forty days with them after His resurrection, going in and out, and eating and drinking: not indeed that He had any experience of hunger and thirst, but even by such evidences confirmed the reality of His flesh, which no longer needed, but still possessed the power, to eat and to drink.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was it, then, these forty days He had in view when He said, “Yet a little while I am with you,” or something else?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it may also be understood in this way: “Yet a little while I am with you;” still, like you, I also am in this state of fleshly infirmity, that is, till He should die and rise again: for after He rose again He was with them, as has been said, for forty days in the full manifestation of His bodily presence; but He was no longer with them in the fellowship of human infirmity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is also another form of His divine presence unknown to mortal senses, of which He likewise says, “Lo, I am with you alway, even to the end of the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, at least, is not the same as “yet a little while I am with you;” for it is not a little while until the end of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or if even this is so (for time flies, and a thousand years are in God’s sight as one day, or as a watch in the night,) yet we cannot believe that He intended any such meaning on this occasion, especially as He went on to say, “Ye shall seek me, and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is to say, after this little while that I am with you, “ye shall seek me, and whither I go, ye cannot come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it after the end of the world that, whither He goes, they will not be able to come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And where, then, is the place of which He is going to say a little after in this same discourse, “Father, I will that they also be with me where I am”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was not then of that presence of His with His own which He is maintaining with them till the end of the world that He now spake, when He said, “Yet a little while I am with you;” but either of that state of mortal infirmity in which He dwelt with them till His passion, or of that bodily presence which He was to maintain with them up till His ascension.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whichever of these any one prefers, he can do so without being at variance with the faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That no one, however, may deem that sense inconsistent with the true one, in which we say that the Lord may have meant the communion of mortal flesh which He held with the disciples till His passion, when He said, “Yet a little while I am with you;” let those words also of His after His resurrection, as found in another evangelist, be taken into consideration, when He said, “These are the words which I spake unto you, while I was yet with you:” as if then He was no longer with them, even at the very time that they were standing by, seeing, touching, and talking with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What does He mean, then, by saying, “while I was yet with you,” but, while I was yet in that state of mortal flesh wherein ye still remain?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then, indeed, He had been raised again in the same flesh; but He was no longer associated with them in the same mortality.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And accordingly, as on that occasion, when now clothed in fleshly immortality, He said with truth, “while I was yet with you,” to which we can attach no other meaning than, while I was yet with you in fleshly mortality; so here also, without any absurdity, we may understand His words, “Yet a little while I am with you,” as if He had said, Yet a little while I am mortal like yourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us look, then, at the words that follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye shall seek me: and as I said unto the Jews, Whither I go, ye cannot come; so say I to you now.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is, ye cannot come now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He said so to the Jews, He did not add the “now.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The former, therefore, were not able at that time to come where He was going, but they were so afterwards; because He says so a little afterwards in the plainest terms to the Apostle Peter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, on the latter inquiring, “Lord, whither goest Thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He replied to him, “Whither I go thou canst not follow me now; but thou shalt follow me afterwards” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what it means is not to be carelessly passed over.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For whither was it that the disciples could not then follow the Lord, but were able afterwards?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we say, to death, what time can be discovered when any one of the sons of men will find it impossible to die; since such, in this perishable body, is the lot of man, that therein life is not a whit easier than death?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were not, therefore, at that time less able to follow the Lord to death, but they were less able to follow Him to the life which is deathless.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thither it was the Lord was going, that, rising from the dead, He should die no more, and death should no more have dominion over Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as the Lord was about to die for righteousness’ sake, how could they have followed Him now, who were as yet unripe for the ordeal of martyrdom?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, with the Lord about to enter the fleshly immortality, how could they have followed Him now, when, even though ready to die, they would have no resurrection till the end of the world?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or, on the point of going, as the Lord was, to the bosom of the Father, and that without any forsaking of them, just as He had never quitted that bosom in coming to them, how could they have followed Him now, since no one can enter on that state of felicity but he that is made perfect in love?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And to show them, therefore, how it is that they may attain the fitness to proceed, where He was going before them, He says, “A new commandment I give unto you, that ye love one another” (ver.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These are the steps whereby Christ must be followed; but any fuller discourse thereon must be put off till another opportunity.
